prompt: An occupied office campus is pursuing LEED O+M recertification. What best describes the typical performance period mindset?

options.text: Submit only as-built drawings from original construction.
Collect and verify operational performance data over a defined performance period.
Skip metering if the building is already occupied.
Wait 5 years after construction before any operations tracking.

explanation: O+M emphasizes ongoing operations data over a defined performance period, not one-time design compliance alone.

distractorExplanations.a: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.c: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.d: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

prompt: Facility managers want early alignment across energy, water, waste, and indoor air for an O+M submission. Which approach is most integrative?

options.text: Hold a cross-functional kickoff to map shared meters, baselines, and credit dependencies.
Assign each credit to a different vendor with no shared data plan.
Document only energy, then address water after certification.
Use marketing claims instead of measured performance.

explanation: Integrative process convenes cross-discipline owners of operations data before credit strategies freeze.

distractorExplanations.b: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.c: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.d: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

prompt: For existing-building operations, which commissioning focus is most appropriate?

options.text: Commission only unfinished design drawings.
Ignore BAS trends if operators say systems 'feel fine.'
Verify that operating systems meet current facility requirements and correct findings.
Defer all functional testing until after recertification.

explanation: Existing buildings emphasize ongoing commissioning / retro-commissioning of systems in use.

distractorExplanations.a: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.b: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.d: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

prompt: Who should own day-to-day evidence for an O+M credit that depends on measured utility data when the IAQ consultant sits outside the core project team?

options.text: Guess values from similar buildings nearby.
Bring the data owner into the project team with clear deliverables and review dates.
Leave the credit blank until GBCI asks questions.
Substitute a brochure for measured data.

explanation: Credit owners must include the people who control meters and logs; integrate them into the team.

distractorExplanations.a: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.c: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.d: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

prompt: Which documentation habit best supports O+M credit review?

options.text: Rely on undated photos without context.
Submit vendor marketing PDFs only.
Use data from a different campus building without disclosure.
Keep dated logs, meter reads, and policies aligned to the performance period.

explanation: Reviewers need traceable performance-period evidence tied to the building and dates.

distractorExplanations.b: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
distractorExplanations.c: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

Maintain a high-SRI roof coating and preserve mature shade trees in parking areas.
Remove trees to maximize asphalt parking.
Add only decorative gravel with no reflectance strategy.

explanation: High-SRI roofs and shade/vegetation reduce heat absorption on existing sites.

distractorExplanations.a: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

prompt: For site management supporting habitat, which practice is preferable?

options.text: Use native or adaptive plantings and minimize unnecessary pesticide use.
Convert all landscaping to invasive ornamentals.

Mow every vegetated buffer to bare soil.

explanation: Native/adaptive plantings and reduced chemical reliance support site ecology in O+M.

distractorExplanations.b: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.

prompt: A campus is adding 2 EV charging stations for O+M transportation credits. What else should the team verify?

explanation: Chargers must be available for occupants/visitors as intended and documented during the performance period.

distractorExplanations.d: This choice misses the LEED O+M requirement or best practice for this credit path.
